title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Compiler Case: Middleware Order Short Circuit
summary: Middleware runs in an unsafe order, async rejections are wrapped twice, error handlers use the wrong signature, dependencies are created per request, and multiple layers write responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts middleware order short circuit increment in composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Enumerate literal, parameter, wildcard, HEAD, OPTIONS, 404, 405, encoded, and near-miss cases before accepting a route table.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Middleware Order Short Circuit · Theory Middleware Order Short Circ…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9672 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
